confirmed.note: Each algorithm gets 4 near-identical harness fns (BM_CPP_X / BM_Lua_X / BM_FakeLua_X_TCC / BM_FakeLua_X_GCC) replicated by hand across ~11 algorithms; the _TCC and _GCC variants are byte-identical except JIT_TCC vs JIT_GCC. ~22 within-file near-dup pairs. Could be one templated helper parameterised on jit-mode.

false_positives.what_happened: Pure SRP code-move refactor: 1961 lines deleted from c_gen.cpp, 1768 added verbatim to new func_body_compiler.cpp (FuncBodyCompiler::*). A naive cross-file chunk detector sees a huge moved block as 'copied between files'. Verified clean: the moved methods do NOT remain in c_gen.cpp afterward, so there is no live duplicate. Not flagged at HEAD, but this is the exact move-refactor that fools cross-file detection.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: when a cross-file match's BASE block is *deleted* in the same commit that ADDs the matching block (net code-move, not duplication), suppress it. Detect by diffing both sides within the same commit: if removed-region == added-region and the source region no longer exists at the commit's tree, classify as 'moved' not 'copied'. Equivalently use git's -M/-C rename/copy detection over hunks before reporting.

false_positives.what_happened: Reverse of 243bc39: 'merge FuncBodyCompiler into CGen' moves ~1950 lines back. Same net-move pattern; a cross-file detector would re-flag the same block as a copy in the opposite direction.
false_positives.fix_proposal: Same move-detection heuristic as above (#056): treat add-here + delete-there in one commit as a move. Additionally keep a per-repo memo of moved blocks so the reverse move is also recognised.

false_positives.what_happened: AST node-class hierarchy: ~20 'class syntax_tree_X final : public syntax_tree_interface' each with a structurally identical ctor 'explicit syntax_tree_X(const syntax_tree_location& loc) : syntax_tree_interface(loc) {}' and an identical-shape 'type() const override { return syntax_tree_type::syntax_tree_type_X; }'. Token/line similarity is high; only the type-enum token differs. This is the polymorphic-node / visitor idiom, not copy-paste debt.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056/archcheck: add an idiom filter for one-line override bodies that consist solely of 'return <Enum>::<member>;' and for trivial single-base-init ctors. Require a minimum count of *non-boilerplate* tokens (strip the enum-member / class-name tokens) before scoring similarity, so single-token-differing polymorphic stubs fall below threshold.

false_positives.what_happened: 'add tcc' vendors the TinyCC compiler: elf.h, dwarf.h, coff.h, arm-tok.h, i386-asm.h, tcc_libm.h etc. plus a prebuilt win_x64 copy of tcc headers. Cross-arch / prebuilt duplicates of vendored compiler headers look like copies between sibling dirs.
false_positives.fix_proposal: archcheck vendor-exclude (#068 graph_vendor_exclude): treat src/platform/tinycc/** and **/prebuilt/** as vendored; exclude from dedup. Heuristic: a dir whose files carry upstream copyright headers (TinyCC / Fabrice Bellard) and is not referenced by the project's own namespace.

false_positives.what_happened: Refactor commit re-touches bison/flex generated parser & lexer (parser.cpp/.h ~1380 lines churn, scanner.cpp). Generated parser tables and skeleton code share large boilerplate regions that a token detector reads as duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: archcheck #065 dedup-skip-generated: detect bison/flex skeleton markers ('A Bison parser, made by GNU Bison', '#line' table-driven blocks, 'yy' symbol prefixes, flex 'generated by flex') and exclude such files, in addition to the existing protobuf/moc/ggml header-marker skip.

cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph on /home/localadm/oss/_aidev_dense/esrrhs_fakelua: nodes=78 edges=201 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1. Consistent with corpus header 'циклы=0(SCC<=1)'. No author-code include cycle to trace.
